Arc Flash Study

CareLabs is an economical supplier of electrical arc flash analysis, investigation, and certification services for businesses of all types. Arc flash risks, employee training, paperwork, and PPE (Personal Protective Equipment) requirements will be assessed by skilled specialists to verify that your electrical safety program is up-to-date, compliant, and comprehensive.  

Get Your Arc Flash Study Report Now

Contact our experts for trustworthy solutions suited to your company's needs.

Enquire Now

CareLabs can discover arc flash risks early on and respond appropriately to mitigate the impact of harmonic distortions to ensure the equipment’s safety. ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program) software is utilized to analyze and investigate Arc flashes. The quantity of light and heat produced by an arc fault is called an arc flash. This occurs when current flows between conductors via an air gap. The arc flash ionizes the air and emits a considerable amount of light and heat (approximately 35,000 degrees Fahrenheit). Arc flash analysis is the process of investigating and analysing the electrical installations of a facility in order to assess the incident energy available for each piece of electrical equipment. By creating safety criteria for competent electricians working on equipment and circuits, arc flash studies encourage safe working conditions. Included also are revised electrical one-line schematics and equipment locations, and a short circuit and coordination analysis. 

Arc Flash Research Objectives:

  • Determine the extent of damage produced by the incident energy emitted during an arc flash fault.
  • Modifying the settings of protective equipment to restrict exposure to incident energy
  • Provide safety recommendations regarding arc flash risks.
  • Follow the specific rules and laws of your country.
  • Determine the type of PPE worn on the job.

The Adverse Consequences of Arc Flash:

  • Arcs result in severe burns to the skin, eyes, and face.
  • The inhalation of gases and hot particles can cause severe burns to the throat and lungs.
  • Flying debris and loose parts cause injury.

Arc flash studies are essential for lowering risks, boosting electrical employees’ safety, and the general safety of the business. Arc flash studies contribute to the identification of electrical hazard levels and the installation of suitable safety measures to limit the risk of burns and accidents. All utilities, manufacturing, industrial, and commercial businesses that use electrical disconnects, motor control, panel boards, and switchboards must do arc flash analysis. 

Arc Flash Research Benefits:

  • Enhanced human and mechanical safety
  • Decreased medical and legal expenses
  • Compliance with regulatory safety standards
  • Reduced violations of norms and penalties

For arc flash analysis, the following procedures are carried out:

  • Data collection
  • Conducting field inspection
  • Conception of a system
  • Analysis of arc flashes

Arc Flash Research Benefits:

  • Enhanced human and mechanical safety
  • Decreased medical and legal expenses
  • Compliance with regulatory safety standards
  • Reduced violations of norms and penalties

For arc flash analysis, the following procedures are carried out:

  • Data collection
  • Conducting field inspection
  • Conception of a system
  • Analysis of arc flashes

CareLabs Work Method Arc Flash Research:

CareLabs delivers exceptional services and will provide any services requested by the construction team, local contractor, or engineering company.

Our work method is comprised of the subsequent steps:

  • Data collection
  • ETAP modelling of a power system
  • Short-circuit examination
  • Coordination of overcurrent protection devices
  • An investigation concerning arc flashes
  • Examination for arc flashes
  • Keeping a ledger
  • Methods to reduce the possibility of an arc flash
  • Full-size one-line diagrams
  • Installation of arc flash markings
  • The finished report

Advantages of Using the Services of CareLabs:

  • Create more secure electrical systems that adhere to regulations.
  • Integrated modules for comprehensive short circuit, overcurrent coordination, device assessment, and arc flash evaluation are combined with protective devices and bus ratings.
  • Creating a more secure workplace
  • Simple alternative suggestion for optimal design
  • Increased safety margins using user-defined arc fault tolerances
  • Our computer-aided analysis will save you time by creating arc flash labels automatically.
  • Reduce productivity losses and maintenance costs.

Crew members are strategically situated throughout CareLabs to ensure that our professionals are close by in case of a regular or urgent situation. CareLabs has rapidly established itself as an ISO 9001:2008-accredited business with a clientele that has supplied outstanding feedback. In addition to São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Brasília, and Salvador, we provide arc flash study and analysis services in all major cities. Contact us immediately for a complimentary estimate on arc flash research and analysis. 

OUR SERVICES

Harmonic Study and Analysis

CareLabs offers electrical installation services such as electrical safety inspections, testing, calibration, and certification, in addition to a comprehensive examination and analysis of harmonics. 

Get Your Harmonic Study Report Now

Contact our experts for trustworthy solutions suited to your company's needs.

Enquire Now

Professionals will analyze harmonic dangers, training programs, paperwork, and personal protective equipment (PPE) to verify that your electrical safety program is up-to-date, compliant, and exhaustive. To ensure the safety of the equipment, CareLabs is able to detect harmonics early on and take the necessary precautions to reduce their influence. Harmonic electric voltages and currents can generate inefficiencies in an electrical system. They produce inefficient heat and can decrease the quality of electricity. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical technique used to forecast resonances and harmonic distortion levels. Harmonic voltage analysis is a mathematical technique for forecasting potential resonances and harmonic distortion levels based on empirical evidence. 

Incorporating harmonic frequencies into the electrical system are nonlinear electrical switching devices such as AC variable frequency drives, DC drives, fluorescent lights, and computers. High harmonic voltage/current levels provide a significant danger of equipment failure. Consequently, a timely harmonic analysis and investigation is essential. Currently, the harmonic limitations for 415-volt systems are 5%, with 4% odd harmonics and 2% even harmonics. 

Effects of Harmonic Distortion:

  • Responsible for high voltage and circulating current.
  • Equipment failure and destruction because of severe voltage distortion
  • A rise in the internal energy of components, which leads to component failure and a shortened lifespan.
  • Fault-induced tripping of branch circuit breakers
  • Measurement flaw
  • Electrical and distribution system fires and explosions
  • Generator failures
  • A reduction in system power factor
  • Installation and utility costs are costly
  • Productivity reductions
  • Inactivity and needless driving
  • Utilization of bigger motors

Harmonic issues are exceedingly rare, but when they do arise, they tend to decrease the power system’s dependability, power quality, and efficiency. Consequently, harmonic investigation and analysis are important. 

  • Protects against current and voltage harmonics
  • Limitation of frequency variance
  • Unbalanced current and voltage
  • Enhance power factor analysis and maintain a value close to one.
  • Regulates voltage fluctuations

By shedding light on the likely cause and effects of power quality problems, harmonic research and analysis will help designers avoid harmonic-related challenges and reduce the possibility of unintended consequences.

Harmonic Study and Analysis Workflow at CareLabs:

  • Highlight available nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and lengthy medium-voltage cables on the electrical single-line diagram.
  • Determine the common coupling point (PCC)
  • Identify system buses inside the facility that are sensitive to harmonic distortions.
  • Gather harmonics information for all nonlinear loads.
  • Obtain vital information on voltage and current harmonics at the selected PCC, including the maximum and lowest short circuit fault levels.
  • Conduct a harmonic analysis of the electrical network using ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program).
  • Determine the overall and individual current and voltage wave distortion values at the buses and PCC.
  • Draw the spectrum of harmonic frequencies.
  • If harmonic distortion levels surpass acceptable thresholds, a suitable mitigation strategy is offered and implemented.
  • After implementing the mitigation solution, rerun the analysis.

Harmonic Study and Analysis Workflow at CareLabs:

  • Highlight available nonlinear loads, capacitor banks, and lengthy medium-voltage cables on the electrical single-line diagram.
  • Determine the common coupling point (PCC)
  • Identify system buses inside the facility that are sensitive to harmonic distortions.
  • Gather harmonics information for all nonlinear loads.
  • Obtain vital information on voltage and current harmonics at the selected PCC, including the maximum and lowest short circuit fault levels.
  • Conduct a harmonic analysis of the electrical network using ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program).
  • Determine the overall and individual current and voltage wave distortion values at the buses and PCC.
  • Draw the spectrum of harmonic frequencies.
  • If harmonic distortion levels surpass acceptable thresholds, a suitable mitigation strategy is offered and implemented.
  • After implementing the mitigation solution, rerun the analysis.

CareLabs Service Benefits:

If background harmonics have previously been measured, the second step consists of higher ratings. 

  • Stage 2 allows for changing converter size based on fault level and converter type if the backdrop is less than 25%.
  • If the backdrop is between 25% and 75%, half of the above converter grade is acceptable.
  • For the higher backdrop, a complete harmonic analysis is conducted.

CareLabs has crew members stationed in many places to ensure that our professionals are close by in case of a regular or urgent situation. CareLabs has swiftly established itself as an ISO 9001:2008-accredited company with a loyal clientele that has supplied outstanding feedback We do harmonic research and analysis in several major Brazilian towns, including São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Brasília, and Salvador Contact us immediately to schedule your harmonic analysis and study in Brazil or to receive a quotation! 

OUR SERVICES

Motor Start Analysis

CareLabs offers organizations in Brazil a specialized Motor start study and analysis solution. The major objective of our motor start analysis service is to assess the atypical effects of starting a big motor.

Get Your Motor Start Analysis Report Now

Contact our experts for trustworthy solutions suited to your company's needs.

Enquire Now

Aside from correctly connecting the electric motor to the line voltage, there are various other criteria to evaluate before starting an electric motor, such as nuisance tripping, high nominal currents, and flickering or fading light, all of which signal a malfunctioning power system.  CareLabs will dispatch skilled professionals to your area to gather current and historical motor installation data. Then, utilizing the collected data and manufacturer input, calculations are done to estimate future implications, and recommendations are made to mitigate the effects. 

Analyzing motor starting strategies may be performed using both static and dynamic simulation methods. We are particularly concerned with research that imitate motor starting circumstances, such as: 

  • Modeling the measured starting impedance of the electric motor yields the load flow solution.
  • For pre-fault short circuit conditions, the short-circuit approach is utilized, whereas the voltage drop method is utilized for motor starting currents.
  • The dynamic model of the electric motor may be used to determine the impact of when an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics is available. motor starting in a conventional transient stability procedure.
  • When detailed dynamic model information for the electric motor is not available, simulations of transient stability can be utilized.

Motor Start Analysis is Required in Brazil:

The utilization of motors in current industrial systems and the size of electric motors are expanding exponentially. Starting a big motor connected to a line can interrupt the electric motor, locally connected loads, and electrically distant buses. When starting an electric motor, the terminal voltage should be at or above 80% of the rated voltage. Voltage difference influences the load torque of a motor because the square of its terminal voltage is directly proportional to the torque. If the voltage drop is significant, machines in operation may reach their breaking torque and decelerate or stop. Voltage drops impacts electrical equipment, sensitive control devices, and lighting loads, among other things. In addition, high beginning current decreases terminal voltage, which causes an increase in the current drawn by all systems supplying a continuous load. Defective undervoltage relays have also been discovered. 

When Should a Motor Start Analysis Be Performed?

  • Prior to or during the acquisition of a huge motor.
  • If no generator is available and the motor rating exceeds 30 percent of the supply transformer’s base KVA rating, the supply transformer will trip
  • If the system is fueled exclusively by the generator and the motor rating surpasses 10 to 15% of the generator’s KVA rating, the generator should be replaced.
  • A motor start study may be necessary if a large number of motors start simultaneously for an industrial operation.
  • Smaller power systems often have low capacity, which magnifies voltage drop difficulties when starting a motor with a large capacity. The analysis of motor beginning facilitates comprehension of the output of tiny generator-containing systems.

When Should a Motor Start Analysis Be Performed?

  • Prior to or during the acquisition of a huge motor.
  • If no generator is available and the motor rating exceeds 30 percent of the supply transformer’s base KVA rating, the supply transformer will trip
  • If the system is fueled exclusively by the generator and the motor rating surpasses 10 to 15% of the generator’s KVA rating, the generator should be replaced.
  • A motor start study may be necessary if a large number of motors start simultaneously for an industrial operation.
  • Smaller power systems often have low capacity, which magnifies voltage drop difficulties when starting a motor with a large capacity. The analysis of motor beginning facilitates comprehension of the output of tiny generator-containing systems.

Analysis of Motor Start Analysis Benefits:

Analysis of motor launch It is advantageous to use a motor or starting method with good speed-torque characteristics. To avoid exceeding the equipment’s torque limitations, any unusual loads must be accelerated under supervision. The motor’s shaft functioning may be analyzed at any moment by performing a motor start study. Consequently, it assists in calculating the equipment’s acceptable torque limitations. Motor starting studies determine the machine’s voltage and whether the electric motor can start and accelerate.

CareLabs offers motor start investigations by skilled professionals to further examine your electric motor. In addition to, we offer motor start analysis services in all major cities in Brazil such as São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Brasília, and Salvador. Reserve your motor start study now or contact us for a quote!

Motor Start Analysis

CareLabs offers organizations in Brazil a specialized Motor start study and analysis solution. The major objective of our motor start analysis service is to assess the atypical effects of starting a big motor.

Get Your Motor Start Analysis Report Now

Contact our experts for trustworthy solutions suited to your company's needs.

Enquire Now

Aside from correctly connecting the electric motor to the line voltage, there are various other criteria to evaluate before starting an electric motor, such as nuisance tripping, high nominal currents, and flickering or fading light, all of which signal a malfunctioning power system.  CareLabs will dispatch skilled professionals to your area to gather current and historical motor installation data. Then, utilizing the collected data and manufacturer input, calculations are done to estimate future implications, and recommendations are made to mitigate the effects. 

Analyzing motor starting strategies may be performed using both static and dynamic simulation methods. We are particularly concerned with research that imitate motor starting circumstances, such as: 

Modeling the measured starting impedance of the electric motor yields the load flow solution.
For pre-fault short circuit conditions, the short-circuit approach is utilized, whereas the voltage drop method is utilized for motor starting currents.
The dynamic model of the electric motor may be used to determine the impact of when an accurate dynamic model of the electric motor circuit and load-torque characteristics is available. motor starting in a conventional transient stability procedure.
When detailed dynamic model information for the electric motor is not available, simulations of transient stability can be utilized.
Motor Start Analysis is Required in Brazil:

The utilization of motors in current industrial systems and the size of electric motors are expanding exponentially. Starting a big motor connected to a line can interrupt the electric motor, locally connected loads, and electrically distant buses. When starting an electric motor, the terminal voltage should be at or above 80% of the rated voltage. Voltage difference influences the load torque of a motor because the square of its terminal voltage is directly proportional to the torque. If the voltage drop is significant, machines in operation may reach their breaking torque and decelerate or stop. Voltage drops impacts electrical equipment, sensitive control devices, and lighting loads, among other things. In addition, high beginning current decreases terminal voltage, which causes an increase in the current drawn by all systems supplying a continuous load. Defective undervoltage relays have also been discovered. 

When Should a Motor Start Analysis Be Performed?
Prior to or during the acquisition of a huge motor.
If no generator is available and the motor rating exceeds 30 percent of the supply transformer’s base KVA rating, the supply transformer will trip
If the system is fueled exclusively by the generator and the motor rating surpasses 10 to 15% of the generator’s KVA rating, the generator should be replaced.
A motor start study may be necessary if a large number of motors start simultaneously for an industrial operation.
Smaller power systems often have low capacity, which magnifies voltage drop difficulties when starting a motor with a large capacity. The analysis of motor beginning facilitates comprehension of the output of tiny generator-containing systems.
Analysis of Motor Start Analysis Benefits:

Analysis of motor launch It is advantageous to use a motor or starting method with good speed-torque characteristics. To avoid exceeding the equipment’s torque limitations, any unusual loads must be accelerated under supervision. The motor’s shaft functioning may be analyzed at any moment by performing a motor start study. Consequently, it assists in calculating the equipment’s acceptable torque limitations. Motor starting studies determine the machine’s voltage and whether the electric motor can start and accelerate.

CareLabs offers motor start investigations by skilled professionals to further examine your electric motor. In addition to, we offer motor start analysis services in all major cities in Brazil such as São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Brasília, and Salvador. Reserve your motor start study now or contact us for a quote!

Power Quality Analysis

Professionals will analyze harmonic dangers, training programs, paperwork, and personal protective equipment (PPE) to verify that your electrical safety program is up-to-date, compliant, and exhaustive.  

Get Your Power Quality Analysis Report Now

Contact our experts for trustworthy solutions suited to your company's needs.

Enquire Now

Numerous organizations and a rising number of individual consumers today utilize data gathering, monitoring, and instrumentation equipment that is extremely vulnerable to power supply changes. Very few electrical items are built to endure the voltage fluctuation spectrum that is a prevalent characteristic of distributed electricity. CareLabs will assist you in resolving power quality concerns related to equipment installation, control, protection, and power conditioning.

Objective of Power Quality Analysis:

  • Evaluation of discrepancies within the electrical system and load.
  • Evaluating the workplace in order to enhance simulation approaches for delivering power quality solutions.
  • Predicting the efficiency and functioning of load equipment
  • Enhance the machine’s power factor and dependability.
  • Avoid excessive energy use.
  • To prevent malfunctions and disturbances.
  • Reducing abnormalities in power quality such as harmonics, spikes, and transients.
  • Help avoid voltage/frequency spikes and PLC lockups.
  • To prevent transformer overheating, capacitor explosion, and nuisance tripping.
  • Reduce significantly utility costs
  • Extend the network’s and its components’ service life.
  • Ensure that electrical equipment can resist changes by inspecting it.

Analysis of Power Quality is Required in Brazil:

All electrical networks face supply disruptions, which are more frequent when storms, natural catastrophes, or accidents destroy power cables. Fluctuations in the power supply are typical.

There are three types of fluctuations, disturbances, and distortions in the properties of power delivered by an electricity network.

1.A frequency deviates from its normal frequency range when it surpasses that range.

2. When the voltage form is kept but the voltage amplitude exceeds the typical range, voltage disruptions develop.

2.1 Voltage drops (dips) and voltage spikes of up to one minute length are examples of fluctuations with a short duration.

2.2 Long duration voltage fluctuations consist of under voltages and over voltages lasting longer than one minute.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage – happens when the load is distributed unevenly across the phases, resulting in substantial equipment heating.

2.4 Differences in voltage between neutral and ground

3. Disturbances resulting from voltage distortion – when the voltage’s shape is not uniform or sinusoidal.

3.1 Harmonics and inter harmonics contribute to device heating, higher losses, inefficient power usage, and resonance issues.

3.2 Transients lasting less than a tenth of a second that are capable of damaging switch contact surfaces, generating system inefficiency, and causing premature failure of motor drives and control.

Analysis of Power Quality is Required in Brazil:

All electrical networks face supply disruptions, which are more frequent when storms, natural catastrophes, or accidents destroy power cables. Fluctuations in the power supply are typical.

There are three types of fluctuations, disturbances, and distortions in the properties of power delivered by an electricity network.

1.A frequency deviates from its normal frequency range when it surpasses that range.

2. When the voltage form is kept but the voltage amplitude exceeds the typical range, voltage disruptions develop.

2.1 Voltage drops (dips) and voltage spikes of up to one minute length are examples of fluctuations with a short duration.

2.2 Long duration voltage fluctuations consist of under voltages and over voltages lasting longer than one minute.

2.3 Unbalanced voltage – happens when the load is distributed unevenly across the phases, resulting in substantial equipment heating.

2.4 Differences in voltage between neutral and ground

3. Disturbances resulting from voltage distortion – when the voltage’s shape is not uniform or sinusoidal.

3.1 Harmonics and inter harmonics contribute to device heating, higher losses, inefficient power usage, and resonance issues.

3.2 Transients lasting less than a tenth of a second that are capable of damaging switch contact surfaces, generating system inefficiency, and causing premature failure of motor drives and control.

What is Included in an Analysis of Power Quality?

Identifying diverse power quality issues necessitates the use of distinct methods, including the following:

  • Harmonic Analysis involves the application of computer algorithms to discover and anticipate prospective harmonic difficulties, as well as reduction approaches.
  • Analysis of Surges and Transients: This investigation will identify surges and transients.
  • This analysis will follow the voltage’s short-term decrease and climb, as well as its value and course.
  • Evaluation of Reactive Force Through this study, the preferred reactive power at the distribution and load ends will be established.
  • This project would measure and synchronize captive electricity to fulfil demand while reducing energy surcharges and fuel usage.
  • This analysis anticipates power flow magnitudes, power factor, voltage levels, and losses in system branches depending on the operational circumstances specified.

CareLabs is committed to offering the highest quality service to enhance the quality and dependability of your electrical installation for the benefit of all of its clients. We offer power quality analysis in all major cities, including São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Brasília, and Salvador. Contact us immediately to schedule an examination of your electricity quality or to obtain a price!

Power System Study and Analysis

CareLabs provides several electrical installation services, such as electrical safety inspections, electrical system design simulations, power quality analysis studies, and power system study and analysis.

Get Your Power System Analysis Report Now

Contact our experts for trustworthy solutions suited to your company's needs.

Enquire Now

Our team of electrical experts use ETAP (Electrical Transient Analysis Program), the most effective tool for power system transient analysis, to conduct load flow and short circuit studies, protective device coordination studies, harmonic emission and filter design studies, transient analysis, and network stability studies. It enables us to work directly with customers to build and execute the most effective power compensation and solutions. 

Why is an Analysis of the Power System Necessary?

Every industrial or commercial establishment needs a consistent, trustworthy, and well-maintained electrical system. Even a slight power loss or failure can have catastrophic repercussions, including longer downtime, damage to vital equipment, and the cost of its restoration. Periodic power system analysis might verify that your facility’s power system is safe, efficient, and dependable under normal and abnormal situations. At each stage of design, installation, and operation, a power system must conduct thorough analysis. Using the ETAP software application, our power system studies and assessments will offer an overview of how the system will perform under normal operation and transient fault circumstances. It describes a set of distinct engineering analytical studies used to study the reaction of an electric power system to events across varying time periods.

Numerous investigations reveal the condition of your facility’s power system. Several examples may be included in a power system analysis research report:

  • Circuit breaker
  • Coordination of safety procedures
  • Transit of Load
  • Dynamic and transient assessment
  • Arc Flash Examination
  • Power Effectiveness
  • Study of harmonics
  • Starting the engine
  • Earthing studies

It helps firms to create a benchmark for other elements of overall plant performance, including the electrical safety program. These investigations indicate the possibility of safe and reliable operations. 

Load Flow Analysis:

Load flow studies provide the computation of voltages and currents in various portions of the network, as well as the selection of equipment with suitable ratings. The built-in load flow function of ETAP may be used to do load flows, which offer information on a power system’s power loading, power factor, voltages, and currents. 

Short-Circuit Investigations:

Short-circuit studies must be conducted on a power system to discover how faulty conditions develop. Performing short circuit studies manually is a time-consuming endeavor, and it is far more efficient to employ competent software like ETAP. ETAP is utilized to simulate system protection devices and assess the impact of three-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures in distribution systems. 

Transient Research:

Studies of transients determine the quantity and forms of transient overvoltage and currents resulting from lightning strikes and switching activities. The outcomes determine the needed quantity of insulation and surge protection. 

Research on Protection Coordination:

This research is designed to validate your system’s numerous security devices. It verifies that they are coordinated and sized appropriately for the equipment they are protecting.

The following essential functions are often incorporated into our power system analysis procedure:

  • Data collecting method
  • Analysis of data and client meetings
  • Accumulating equipment data
  • Model of a single-line diagram
  • ETAP power system modelling
  • Analysis of short circuit and load flow
  • Investigations into protection cooperation
  • Arc flash evaluation
  • A summary of the study report.
  • Risk assessment and mitigation planning
  • Project administration

Load Flow Analysis:

Load flow studies provide the computation of voltages and currents in various portions of the network, as well as the selection of equipment with suitable ratings. The built-in load flow function of ETAP may be used to do load flows, which offer information on a power system’s power loading, power factor, voltages, and currents. 

Short-Circuit Investigations:

Short-circuit studies must be conducted on a power system to discover how faulty conditions develop. Performing short circuit studies manually is a time-consuming endeavor, and it is far more efficient to employ competent software like ETAP. ETAP is utilized to simulate system protection devices and assess the impact of three-phase, L-G, L-L, and L-L-G failures in distribution systems. 

Transient Research:

Studies of transients determine the quantity and forms of transient overvoltage and currents resulting from lightning strikes and switching activities. The outcomes determine the needed quantity of insulation and surge protection. 

Research on Protection Coordination:

This research is designed to validate your system’s numerous security devices. It verifies that they are coordinated and sized appropriately for the equipment they are protecting. 

The following essential functions are often incorporated into our power system analysis procedure:

  • Data collecting method
  • Analysis of data and client meetings
  • Accumulating equipment data
  • Model of a single-line diagram
  • ETAP power system modelling
  • Analysis of short circuit and load flow
  • Investigations into protection cooperation
  • Arc flash evaluation
  • A summary of the study report.
  • Risk assessment and mitigation planning
  • Project administration

The Following Components Make Up the CareLabs Power System Analysis:

  • Possibilities for reducing energy prices and consumption.
  • Highlighting issue areas in reports.
  • Suggestions for solutions
  • Details on how and where energy is utilized.
  • Verification of safety device ratings
  • Assist in preventing damage and injury.
  • The effects of harmonics and transients

Our power system analysis studies concentrate on evaluating the client’s electrical system and recommending ways to improve its performance and supply security. São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Brasília, and Salvador are among the key cities where we provide power system analysis and study services. Contact us immediately to schedule a study and analysis of your power system or to obtain a price!

Transient Research:

Studies of transients determine the quantity and forms of transient overvoltage and currents resulting from lightning strikes and switching activities. The outcomes determine the needed quantity of insulation and surge protection. 

Research on Protection Coordination:

This research is designed to validate your system’s numerous security devices. It verifies that they are coordinated and sized appropriately for the equipment they are protecting. 

The following essential functions are often incorporated into our power system analysis procedure:

Data collecting method
Analysis of data and client meetings
Accumulating equipment data
Model of a single-line diagram
ETAP power system modelling
Analysis of short circuit and load flow
Investigations into protection cooperation
Arc flash evaluation
A summary of the study report.
Risk assessment and mitigation planning
Project administration
The Following Components Make Up the CareLabs Power System Analysis:
Possibilities for reducing energy prices and consumption.
Highlighting issue areas in reports.
Suggestions for solutions
Details on how and where energy is utilized.
Verification of safety device ratings
Assist in preventing damage and injury.
The effects of harmonics and transients

Our power system analysis studies concentrate on evaluating the client’s electrical system and recommending ways to improve its performance and supply security. São Paulo, Rio de Janeiro, Brasília, and Salvador are among the key cities where we provide power system analysis and study services. Contact us immediately to schedule a study and analysis of your power system or to obtain a price!
